disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Exertional dyspnoea specifically when walking to the bathroom, in addition to fluttering palpitations and fatigue
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any shortness of breath with this?
Does moving around make you more breathless?
How is your breathing been with the palpitations?
Any breathlessness when you get up and move?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, actually — when I got up to walk to the bathroom I noticed I was more short of breath than I'd expect. It settled a bit once I sat back down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No focal neurological symptoms — no facial droop, slurred speech, limb weakness, or visual disturbance suggestive of stroke/TIA
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any weakness or numbness on one side of your face, arm, or leg?
Any trouble speaking, or has your speech seemed slurred to you or anyone else?
Any changes in your vision or sudden weakness anywhere?
Any signs of a stroke — face drooping, arm weakness, or difficulty speaking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no weakness, no trouble talking, my face feels normal. Just the racing heart and feeling washed out.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so, but I'm not sure what to look for.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is not currently taking any blood-thinning or anticoagulant medication
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any blood thinners or anticoagulant medication, like warfarin or a DOAC?
Have you ever been on medication to thin your blood?
Do you take aspirin or any other blood-thinning tablets regularly?
Any history of being prescribed a blood thinner?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not on anything like that — just the blood pressure tablet.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so, just my usual tablet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain or chest tightness accompanying the palpitations
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any chest pain or tightness with this?
Any pain in your chest at all, separate from the fluttering feeling?
Is there any chest discomfort along with the racing feeling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, just the fluttering and feeling washed out and a bit lightheaded.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do you think I need to go to hospital, or can I just rest here at home? I really don't want to make a fuss.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledge the patient's preference to stay home while explaining that a new, sudden irregular heart rhythm with lightheadedness needs prompt assessment — including an ECG — to check it is safe to manage as an outpatient, rather than simply reassuring her to rest without further evaluation.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Will I need to be on medication long term for this? I don't love the idea of being on tablets forever.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain honestly that new-onset atrial fibrillation often requires both rate-control and stroke-prevention (blood-thinning) medication, and that this will be properly assessed and started by the treating clinician after ECG confirmation and a stroke-risk score — do not minimise the likely need for ongoing anticoagulation given her age and hypertension.

deflection_behaviours.description: When asked about her support at home, patient mentions feeling anxious about living alone before answering directly — tests whether the AI Doctor acknowledges the social context while still gathering the clinical detail needed.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I do live alone, and if I'm honest this dizzy feeling has made me a bit nervous being by myself... but anyway, to answer your question, yes it's still there.
